taskDescription: Drag the correct neuroanatomical terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph about the nervous system.
textField:
The nervous system is divided into the *central* nervous system (brain and spinal cord) and the *peripheral* nervous system (cranial and spinal nerves). The CNS is protected by the *meninges*, cerebrospinal fluid, and the blood-brain barrier.

The autonomic nervous system has two divisions: the *sympathetic* (fight or flight) from T1-L2 and the *parasympathetic* (rest and digest) from craniosacral outflow. Sympathetic preganglionic fibres are short and synapse in the *paravertebral* or prevertebral ganglia.

In the CNS, myelination is performed by *oligodendrocytes*, while in the PNS it is performed by *Schwann* cells. Each oligodendrocyte can myelinate *multiple* axon segments, whereas each Schwann cell wraps only one internode.

A typical spinal nerve is formed by the union of a *dorsal* root (sensory) and a *ventral* root (motor). The cell bodies of sensory neurons are located in the *dorsal root ganglion*.

An upper motor neuron lesion produces *spastic* paralysis with increased reflexes, while a lower motor neuron lesion produces *flaccid* paralysis with decreased reflexes and *fasciculations*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the organisation of the nervous system.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
Excellent understanding of the nervous system!
